assumptions: handshake_signals is a flattened compatibility view derived from handshake_groups.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: All enrichments and new cases respect the signal set defined in the contract.
LLM planning note: No exact-cycle latency assumptions are introduced; observations are driven by the external done flag.
LLM planning note: Negative case targets the unresolved reset/start polarity ambiguity by exercising start during active reset.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk oracle hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it reinforced already-known contract or plan constraints.

checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'reset_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: Guardrail: avoid fixed-cycle expectations unless the contract later proves them.
notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires conservative, externally visible progress or stability and forbids hidden exact-cycle assumptions.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: Ensure that the done flag does not assert more than once between two distinct start events.
checks.pass_condition: For any pair of consecutive start assertions, the done signal may transition from 0 to 1 at most once. Any additional 1→0→1 cycles without an intervening start constitute a violation.
checks.trigger_condition: When a start event is observed, monitor done until the next start event.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'edge_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: done cannot be treated as completion without a prior visible start event.
notes: Avoids false positives from orphan done pulses or misinterpreted combinational outputs.
checks.pass_condition: A completion claim requires an earlier start event in the same scenario; exact-cycle completion is not required.
checks.trigger_condition: Whenever completion is claimed for the linked start/done interaction.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'protocol_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: repeated operations must not collapse into a single ambiguous completion claim.
notes: Stability-style property used instead of throughput-specific claims.
checks.pass_condition: Observed progress remains order-aware and non-contradictory even when exact queueing semantics are unresolved.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked case applies repeated or back-to-back operations.

unresolved_items: Property oracle for case 'back_to_back_001' is acting as a guardrail because stronger functional semantics are not yet justified.

checks.description: Guardrail: illegal or constrained inputs do not require normal completion or success signaling.
notes: Negative behavior is guarded by explicit contract constraints only.
checks.pass_condition: The oracle only requires safe non-violation behavior and does not invent undocumented error responses.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan probes documented illegal or constrained inputs.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'rst_n' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Observe completion only after a visible start event has occurred.
notes: Completion-safe oracle uses event ordering, not cycle counting.
checks.pass_condition: 'done' may be treated as completion only after the start event; no predetermined completion latency is assumed.
checks.trigger_condition: When 'start' is pulsed or asserted for a legal operation.

notes: Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

checks.description: Require repeated operations to be distinguished by separate protocol-visible acceptance or completion events.
notes: Repeated-operation oracle avoids throughput and queue-depth assumptions.
checks.pass_condition: A second operation is not treated as accepted or complete unless a distinct later protocol-visible event is observed.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies repeated or back-to-back operations with minimal spacing.

unresolved_items: Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: If the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is reset to 0.
Reset polarity for 'start' remains ambiguous; negative case explicitly tests start while reset is asserted.
Spec hint about shift‑count reset at i==17 is not mapped to an external port and therefore not directly testable.
Contract or plan confidence is limited; oracle generation is intentionally conservative and may omit value-level functional checks.
